info.name: Bank Challenge — Microservices API
info.description:
Complete API validation package for the banking technical challenge (Senior level).

Microservices:
  customer-service → http://localhost:8081
  account-service  → http://localhost:8080

Recommended execution order:
  1. Clientes        — create PDF use-case customers
  2. Cuentas         — create PDF use-case accounts
  3. Movimientos     — execute PDF movements
  4. Reportes        — validate report endpoint
  5. Negative Scenarios — validate error handling

Collection variables are captured automatically by test scripts (joseLemaId, marianelaMontalvoId, accountId_478758, etc.).

item.name: Clientes
item.description:
CRUD for /clientes — customer-service (port 8081).
Run all Create requests first so IDs are captured before Cuentas.

exec: pm.test('Response has id — captured as joseLemaId', function () {
pm.test('Customer name is Jose Lema', function () {
pm.test('Response has id — captured as marianelaMontalvoId', function () {
pm.test('Customer name is Marianela Montalvo', function () {
pm.test('Response has id — captured as juanOsorioId', function () {
pm.test('Customer name is Juan Osorio', function () {

item.name: Create Temp Customer (Delete Demo)
item.description: Creates a disposable customer used only to demonstrate DELETE. Safe to delete without affecting the PDF use-case flow.

exec: pm.test('Response contains all expected customer fields', function () {

item.name: Update Customer (Jose Lema — change address)
item.description: Full update. All fields required. Demonstrates PUT /clientes/{id}.

exec: pm.test('Name and status remain unchanged', function () {

item.name: Delete Customer (Temp)
item.description: Deletes the disposable customer created by 'Create Temp Customer'. Does not affect PDF use-case customers.

item.name: Cuentas
item.description:
CRUD for /cuentas — account-service (port 8080).
Run after Clientes so customers are already created.
customerName is resolved to customer ID by the account-service via GET /clientes/buscar?nombre={name}.

exec: pm.test('Account id captured as accountId_478758', function () {
pm.test('Account id captured as accountId_225487', function () {
pm.test('Account id captured as accountId_495878', function () {
pm.test('Account id captured as accountId_496825', function () {

item.name: Create Account 585545 - Corriente - Jose Lema
item.description: PDF use case step 3: new Corriente account for Jose Lema.

exec: pm.test('Account id captured as accountId_585545', function () {
pm.test('At least 5 accounts exist (PDF use cases)', function () {
pm.test('Response contains all account fields', function () {

item.name: Update Account (478758 — change accountType)
item.description: UpdateAccountRequest supports accountType and status. Demonstrates PUT /cuentas/{id}.

exec: pm.test('Account type was updated to Ahorros', function () {

item.name: Delete Account (585545)
item.description: Deletes account 585545 (Jose Lema Corriente). No movements were registered on this account in the PDF flow, so deletion is safe.

item.name: Movimientos
item.description:
Movement registration for /movimientos — account-service (port 8080).

MovementRequest: { accountId: Long, amount: BigDecimal }
  Positive amount → Deposito
  Negative amount → Retiro

PDF use-case movements (step 4):
  478758 Retiro 575   → balance: 2000 - 575 = 1425
  225487 Deposito 600 → balance: 100 + 600 = 700
  495878 Deposito 150 → balance: 0 + 150 = 150
  496825 Retiro 540   → balance: 540 - 540 = 0

exec: pm.test('Movement id captured as movementId', function () {

item.name: Get Movement by ID
item.description: Fetches the first movement created (Retiro 575 on account 478758). ID was captured in movementId variable.

exec: pm.test('Response contains all movement fields', function () {

item.name: Get Movements by Account (225487)
item.description: GET /movimientos?accountId={id} — returns all movements for a given account.

exec: pm.test('At least one movement for account 225487', function () {

item.name: Reportes
item.description:
Report endpoint — account-service (port 8080).

Endpoint: GET /reportes?clienteId={id}&fechaInicio={date}&fechaFin={date}
Date format: ISO (yyyy-MM-dd)

Response fields match PDF example exactly:
  Fecha, Cliente, Numero Cuenta, Tipo, Saldo Inicial, Estado, Movimiento, Saldo Disponible

Note: The PDF specifies /reportes?fecha=rango fechas. The implementation uses
clienteId + fechaInicio + fechaFin query params for precision and type safety.

item.name: Reporte Estado Cuenta — Marianela Montalvo
item.description:
Generates account statement for Marianela Montalvo.
Expected result mirrors the PDF example:
  225487 Corriente: Deposito 600, Saldo Disponible 700
  496825 Ahorros:   Retiro -540, Saldo Disponible 0

exec: pm.test('Report lines have all PDF-required fields', function () {
pm.test('All lines belong to Marianela Montalvo', function () {
pm.test('Deposito on 225487: Saldo Disponible is 700', function () {
pm.test('Retiro on 496825: Saldo Disponible is 0', function () {

item.name: Negative Scenarios
item.description:
Error handling validation:
  - Insufficient balance must return exactly 'Saldo no disponible' (HTTP 400)
  - Account creation with non-existent customer returns 404
  - GET on non-existent resources returns 404
  - Zero-amount movement returns 400

These requests can be run independently of the PDF flow order.

item.name: Insufficient Balance — Saldo no disponible
item.description: Attempts to withdraw 999999 from account 478758 (max balance 2000 even before movements). Must return HTTP 400 with exact message 'Saldo no disponible'.

exec: pm.test('Error message is exactly Saldo no disponible', function () {
pm.test('Error response has status field', function () {

item.name: Zero Amount Movement — Rejected
item.description: amount: 0 must be rejected with HTTP 400 and a descriptive error (InvalidAmountException).

item.name: Create Account with Non-existent Customer
item.description: account-service calls customer-service to resolve customerName. A non-existent name must return 404 from the integration boundary.

exec: pm.test('Error message mentions customer not found', function () {
pm.test('Error body has status and message fields', function () {
pm.test('Error body has status and message fields', function () {

item.name: Create Account with Missing Required Fields — 400
item.description: accountNumber is @NotBlank. Sending an empty string triggers bean validation and returns 400.

exec: pm.test('Validation error message is present', function () {

item.name: Create Customer with Missing Required Fields — 400
item.description: name is @NotBlank. Omitting it triggers bean validation and returns 400.

exec: pm.test('Validation error message is present', function () {
